concept.math_concept: Two basketball-themed primitives — shot trajectory (parabolic flight) and bouncing-ball decay (geometric sequence) — authored together for an upcoming demo.
concept.real_world_analog: Basketball shot mechanics (release angle + arc + rim) and ball-bounce energy loss (each bounce returns a fraction of previous height — coefficient of restitution).

narration.script: Every basketball shot is a parabola. The release point, the peak, and the rim — three points that uniquely determine the curve. And every bounce after the shot is geometric decay — each rebound returning a fixed fraction of the previous height, the sequence converging toward zero.

beats.narration_span: Every basketball shot is a parabola. The release point, the peak, and the rim — three points that uniquely determine the curve.

visual.primary: Basketball shot trajectory — wireframe ball arcing from a shooter's release point to the rim of a hoop, with the parabolic path visible as a dashed trail and the rim + net rendered as a clean wireframe diagram.

_new.description: Wireframe basketball shot showing a ball arc from a release point to the rim of a basketball hoop. The ball follows a parabolic path with the rim drawn as a clean circle + short net hatching beneath it. A dashed trail traces past ball positions so the parabola is legible at any frame.
_new.use_case: Quadratic equation / projectile motion / parabola intro videos — where you want a recognizable real-world shot rather than an abstract curve. The arc serves as the focal point; the rim anchors the math to a concrete outcome.

props_schema.release_position: [number, number] — pixel coords of the shooter's release point. Default [200, 600] (lower-left of canvas).
props_schema.rim_position: [number, number] — pixel coords of the rim center. Default [1000, 350] (upper-right).
props_schema.peak_height_pixels: number — how high above the rim the apex of the arc sits. Default 200. Controls the parabola's height; ball must remain ≥ 100px from canvas top.
props_schema.show_trail: boolean — draw a dashed trail of past ball positions so the parabola reads at every frame. Default true.
props_schema.show_court_floor: boolean — draw a faint horizontal floor line at the release y-coord, contextualizing the shot. Default true.
props_schema.ball_radius: number — basketball radius in pixels. Default 18. Drawn with a single horizontal seam line + a single vertical seam line so it reads as a basketball, not just a circle.

overlays.content: Every basketball shot is a parabola.

beats.narration_span: And every bounce after the shot is geometric decay — each rebound returning a fixed fraction of the previous height, the sequence converging toward zero.

visual.primary: Basketball bouncing on a court floor with each successive rebound reaching a lower peak — visualizing geometric decay. Each peak labeled with its decayed height; floor line + bounce-position dots indicate impact points.

_new.description: Wireframe basketball bouncing on a horizontal floor line, with each rebound reaching `restitution` fraction of the previous peak height. The path is the canonical geometric-decay-of-peaks visualization (h, h·r, h·r², h·r³, …). Optional height labels at each peak make the geometric sequence explicit. The ball moves horizontally with a constant speed across the bounces.
_new.use_case: Geometric sequences / exponential decay / coefficient-of-restitution intro videos. The shrinking peaks ARE the math being introduced; labels at each peak surface the sequence values.

props_schema.initial_height_pixels: number — starting drop height above the floor in pixels. Default 380.
props_schema.restitution: number in (0, 1) — fraction of height retained per bounce. Default 0.7. Each successive peak is `restitution × previous_peak`.
props_schema.num_bounces: number — how many bounce arcs to show. Default 5.
props_schema.show_trail: boolean — draw a dashed trail of past ball positions so the full parabolic-sequence pattern reads at every frame. Default true.
props_schema.show_heights: boolean — label each peak with its height value (h₀, h₁ = h₀·r, h₂ = h₀·r², …) so the geometric structure is explicit. Default true.
props_schema.ball_radius: number — basketball radius in pixels. Default 18. Drawn with crossing seams so it reads as a basketball.
props_schema.horizontal_speed_pixels_per_s: number — how fast the ball travels horizontally across the floor while bouncing. Default 80 px/s.

_meta.purpose: Seed two basketball primitives (basketball_shot, basketball_bounce) for an upcoming demo where the user really likes basketball.
_meta.note: Author flow runs against this brief via --brief-file. TTS/render can be skipped; the value is the primitives that land on disk.
